AI.rationale: MSFT Q3 FY26 Azure +40% YoY, AI annualized $37B (+123% YoY), 20M+ Copilot seats, FY26 capex $190B; GOOGL Q1 2026 Cloud +63% YoY, $460B backlog (~doubled QoQ), gen AI revenue +800% YoY, 2026 capex $180-190B; NVDA $5T market cap Apr 24, FY26 revenue $215.94B (+65%), $1T Blackwell+Rubin pipeline through 2027. Production-scale adoption with peak-adjacent valuations.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: RKLB closed Mynaric acquisition Apr 14 ($155M) — laser optical comms vertical integration into SDA PWSA; Space Force missile defense program selection. Neutron tank rupture pushed first launch to Q4 2026, dev cost $360M. Real revenue + government contracts; pre-Neutron-debut.

robotics.rationale: China humanoid output projected +94% in 2026 (TrendForce Apr); Unitree targeting 20K units 2026 at ~$13,500. ISRG (~5.8% BOTZ) Q1 +23% revenue, raised guidance. Beijing humanoid half-marathon (Alibaba/Unitree) real-world demo. Adoption compounding, near-commercial humanoid viability.

engineered_biology.rationale: Intellia Phase 3 HAELO win Apr 27 — first ever Phase 3 success for in vivo CRISPR (87% swelling-attack reduction, no SAEs in active arm), rolling BLA targeting 1H 2027 approval. NTLA -15% on $180M dilution despite the win. Science de-risked, commercial revenue 1-2 years away.

quantum.rationale: QTUM crossed $4B AUM Apr 27 (5-star Morningstar, 44.88% TTM, 354.76% since 2018 inception); IonQ photonic interconnect milestone Apr 14 (first networked trapped-ion quantum systems). Institutional holder Breakthru trimmed QTUM by $5.9M after 75% gain — late-buildup/early-surge dynamic. All quantum companies still pre-profit.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: Commonwealth Fusion applied to PJM grid Apr 30 — first-ever for private fusion; demo tokamak 75% built (operations late 2027), 400MW Fall Line plant target. HANetf European NUKZ launched Apr 2; NRC opened 90-day fusion regulatory framework comment period. NUKZ April +11.6%, $432M 1-yr inflow. Funded buildup; commercial fusion 3-5+ years away.

general_markets.rationale: Fed held 3.50-3.75% Apr 29 third consecutive (8-4 vote, most divided since Oct 1992); $31.5B bond ETF inflows in April on tariff/Liberation Day risk-off; gold record above $5,500 then mid-month pullback (IAU April flat). VIG -1.75% Q1 underperformance.
